tables.table_note: PDR-style reweighting improves over uniform aggregation, consistent with Liu et al. (2026). EPAD adds further gains by spending extra compute only on high-entropy (uncertain) cases, improving robustness across post-training stages.

tables.table_title: Efficiency: compute reduction via entropy gating (↓ is better)
tables.table_note: Similar to L-RAG’s tunable trade-off (Voloshyn, 2026), EPAD exposes a knob \(\tau\) controlling compute/accuracy. Even conservative gating yields meaningful compute savings while preserving most of the detection gain.

headers: Score variance across stages (lower is more stable)
tables.table_note: Because SFT and RL are theoretically non-decoupled and empirically degrade each other’s objective (Niu et al., 2026), detectors can drift across stages. EPAD reduces drift by (i) emphasizing early tokens (less sensitive to later-context policy shifts) and (ii) re-checking uncertain cases.
